title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei wants AI models regulated like airplanes
snippet: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has said there is a stricter need for regulation around AI, similar to how Airplanes are regulated.

title: Former xAI engineer claims he was fired for raising safety concerns about Grok, told ‘AI will kill us all anyway’
snippet: A former xAI engineer has sued Elon Musk's xAI and SpaceX, alleging he was fired for raising safety concerns about the Grok AI chatbot.

title: Not even the iPhone 17 can run Apple's newest on-device AI models: Check eligibility list
snippet: Apple's latest advanced AI features will only be available on newer models including iPhone 17 Pro, Pro Max and iPhone Air. This is because Apple now requires minimum 12GB of RAM for on-device AI models.&nbsp;

title: New leap for embodied AI: Hong Kong to launch 24-hour convenience store run entirely by a humanoid robot
snippet: Embodied AI is transforming various industries, including retail. A new store in Hong Kong will be operated by a humanoid robot, offering 24-hour service. This initiative follows successful implementations in factories and other sectors, showcasing AI's growing role in everyday life.

title: Did Apple just leak its foldable iPhone? iOS 27's leaked code suggests so
snippet: Apple hinted at its first foldable phone during the iOS 27 launch, revealing code that tracks device folding states.&nbsp;

title: LSEG slowly sheds 'AI risk' tag with drive to show growth
snippet: LSEG shares tumbled nearly 13% in one day in February as worries about the threat posed by large language artificial intelligence models like Anthropic's Claude triggered a sharp selloff in software stocks.

title: TCS and Anthropic launch global premier partnership to drive enterprise AI scaling
snippet: Under the deal, TCS becomes a Global Premier Partner in Anthropic's Claude Partner Network. The company will deploy Claude to 50,000 of its employees across functions like engineering, finance, legal, and sales. TCS said this internal rollout will help it build hands-on expertise before applying it

title: AI risks are here, time for tough rules: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei
snippet: As artificial intelligence grows more powerful, governments must move beyond transparency measures and introduce stronger regulations to address emerging risks, according to Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei.

title: South Korea fines Coupang $409 million in country's largest data breach penalty
snippet: The Personal Information Protection Commission said the New York-listed company had leaked personal data of more than 33 million customers and failed to detect the breach within the 72 hours required by the law.

title: China's control over indium phosphide exports threatens AI data centre rollout
snippet: Anderson's visit was partly ​to raise the issue of delays in China's export licenses involving the highly strategic material, essential in manufacturing high-speed optical chips for AI data centres, said three sources ​familiar with the matter.

title: Canada introduces legislation to ban social media for children under 16, regulate AI chatbots
snippet: The bill also aims to make AI chatbots safer by setting up a digital regulator to establish safety standards, a government official said. Companies could face penalties of 3% of global revenue or up to C$10 million ($7.2 million), whichever is more, for failing to comply.

title: OpenAI considers drastic price cuts, anticipating war for users with Anthropic: Report

title: OpenAI says Chinese propaganda is being deployed to foment dissent over tariffs, data centers
snippet: The company said its efforts, dating to late 2025 and early 2026, appeared to have had little or no effect, but it is another indication of how central generative AI is ‌becoming in digital ⁠influence ⁠campaigns - even ones aimed against AI companies themselves.
